item.title: WHP awards 90 Ticino companies for workplace health
item.description: ATS Insubria and Confindustria Varese recognize companies that promote health in the workplace with the WHP program
item.content:encoded:
## Awarding of Virtuous Companies

ATS Insubria and Confindustria Varese have awarded nearly 90 companies in the area for their adherence to the WHP (Workplace Health Promotion) program. This program aims to promote health and well-being in the workplace, an initiative that involves both Italian and Swiss companies, particularly in the Canton of Ticino.

### The WHP Program

The WHP program is an initiative that encourages companies to implement health and well-being policies for their employees. The awarded companies have demonstrated a concrete commitment to improving working conditions by introducing well-being programs, physical activities, and psychological support. This recognition is an important signal for companies operating in the border area between Italy and Switzerland, where collaboration between public and private entities is fundamental.

The awarded companies come from various sectors, including manufacturing, services, and commerce. The award ceremony took place in Varese, with the participation of representatives from local institutions and the awarded companies. This event represents an important opportunity to exchange experiences and best practices among the participating companies.

### The Importance of Health at Work

Promoting health in the workplace is fundamental to improving productivity and the well-being of employees. Companies that adhere to the WHP program not only benefit from a healthier work environment but also contribute to creating a positive corporate culture. This is particularly relevant for cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy, where the quality of life and work is a priority.

The awarded companies have implemented a series of initiatives, including fitness programs, health training courses, and psychological support services. These initiatives not only improve the quality of life for employees but also contribute to reducing absenteeism and increasing job satisfaction.

### The Awarded Companies

Among the awarded companies are some of the most important economic realities in the area, operating in key sectors such as industry, commerce, and services. The complete list of awarded companies is available on the website of Confindustria Varese and ATS Insubria. This recognition is an important milestone for companies that have demonstrated a concrete commitment to promoting the health and well-being of their employees.

The awarded companies have received a recognition certificate and will be included in a public list of virtuous companies. This recognition is an important incentive for other companies to follow their example and adhere to the WHP program.

### Impact on the Territory

The initiative has a significant impact on the territory, promoting a culture of health and well-being that involves both companies and employees. This is particularly relevant for cross-border workers in Ticino, where the quality of life and work is a priority. The awarded companies represent a positive model for other economic realities in the area, demonstrating that it is possible to reconcile productivity and employee well-being.

The WHP program is an example of how collaboration between public and private entities can lead to concrete results for the well-being of workers. This recognition is an important step forward in promoting a culture of health and well-being in the workplace, contributing to creating a healthier and more productive work environment.

### Conclusions

The awarding of 90 companies in the area by ATS Insubria and Confindustria Varese represents an important recognition for companies that promote health and well-being in the workplace. This program is an example of how collaboration between public and private entities can lead to concrete results for the well-being of workers, particularly for cross-border workers operating in the Canton of Ticino.

The awarded companies represent a positive model for other economic realities in the area, demonstrating that it is possible to reconcile productivity and employee well-being. This recognition is an important incentive for other companies to follow their example and adhere to the WHP program, contributing to creating a healthier and more productive work environment. ## Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who commute to Ticino and reside in Italy, joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work. Companies that join the program offer a series of benefits to their employees, including fitness programs, health training courses, and psychological support services.

### Benefits for employees

Employees of companies that join the WHP program benefit from a series of initiatives aimed at improving their health and well-being. These programs include:

- Fitness programs and physical activities
- Health and well-being training courses
- Psychological support services
- Initiatives for stress prevention and management
- Healthy eating and nutrition programs

These benefits not only improve the quality of life for employees but also help reduce absenteeism and increase job satisfaction. For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, these programs represent an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the introduction of the WHP program, many companies did not have health and well-being policies for their employees. This led to a series of problems, including increased absenteeism, reduced productivity, and greater job dissatisfaction. The introduction of the WHP program represented a significant step forward in improving the quality of life and work for employees.

Companies that have joined the WHP program have demonstrated a concrete commitment to promoting the health and well-being of their employees. This has led to a series of benefits, including reduced absenteeism, increased productivity, and greater job satisfaction. For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, this represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work.

### Future scenarios

Joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity for companies operating in the border area between Italy and Switzerland. Companies that join the program benefit from a series of advantages, including improved quality of life and work for their employees, reduced absenteeism, and increased productivity.

For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work. Companies that join the program offer a series of benefits to their employees, including fitness programs, health training courses, and psychological support services. This represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers to improve their quality of life and work.

### Conclusion

Joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity for companies operating in the border area between Italy and Switzerland. Companies that join the program benefit from a series of advantages, including improved quality of life and work for their employees, reduced absenteeism, and increased productivity. For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do to join the WHP program

To join the WHP program, companies must follow a series of steps. These steps include submitting an application, evaluating the health and well-being initiatives implemented, and participating in a certification process.

### Steps to join

1. Submit the application
2. Evaluation of the health and well-being initiatives implemented
3. Participation in the certification process
4. Recognition and award

Companies wishing to join the WHP program must submit an application to the Confindustria Varese or ATS Insubria website. The application must include a description of the health and well-being initiatives implemented by the company.

### Evaluation of initiatives

The health and well-being initiatives implemented by the company will be evaluated by a commission of experts. The commission will evaluate the quality and effectiveness of the initiatives implemented, as well as their impact on the health and well-being of employees. Companies that achieve a sufficiently high score will be invited to participate in the certification process.

### Certification process

Companies participating in the certification process must demonstrate that they have implemented a series of health and well-being initiatives for their employees. These initiatives must be consistent with the criteria of the WHP program and must be positively evaluated by the commission of experts. Companies that obtain certification will be awarded during an official ceremony.

### Recognition and award

Companies that obtain WHP certification will be awarded during an official ceremony. Awarded companies will receive a certificate of recognition and will be included in a public list of virtuous companies. This recognition is an important incentive for other companies to follow their example and join the WHP program.

### Useful tools

For more information on the WHP program and to submit an application, companies can visit the Confindustria Varese or ATS Insubria website. These sites contain detailed information about the program, evaluation criteria, and steps to join.

In addition, companies can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate the impact of health and well-being initiatives on their employees. This tool can help companies plan and implement effective and sustainable health and well-being initiatives.

### Conclusion

Joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity for companies operating in the border area between Italy and Switzerland. Companies that join the program benefit from a series of advantages, including an improvement in the quality of life and work of their employees, a reduction in absenteeism, and an increase in productivity. For cross-border workers in Ticino, joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/21/leggi-notizia/argomenti/economia-13/articolo/promuovere-la-salute-nei-luoghi-di-lavoro-ats-insubria-e-confindustria-varese-premiano-quasi-90-az.html)*

item.title: BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026: three routes for everyone
item.description: On Sunday, April 26, 2026, in Mendrisio, three routes of varying difficulty, a free lunch, and entertainment for the new edition of BancaStato Walking Mendrisio.
item.content:encoded:
On Sunday, April 26, 2026, the BancaStato Walking Mendrisio event returns, taking place at the Centro Manifestazioni Mercato Coperto in Mendrisio. The 2026 edition offers three routes of varying difficulty: Family (3.6 km), Castle (9.2 km), and Salorino (12.1 km). The Family route is flat and accessible to everyone, including those with strollers. The Castle route, of medium difficulty, winds through the countryside and vineyards of the municipalities of Castello S. Pietro and Salorino. The Salorino route, the most challenging, offers a spectacular panorama and passes through Corteglia, Castel S. Pietro, Obino, Salorino, and Somazzo.

### 📊 Route Details

- **Family (3.6 km)**: Ideal for families and beginners, this route is completely flat and passes through the center of Mendrisio, the Parco delle Valli, and Lake Mendrisio. The average time to complete the route is about 45 minutes.
- Castle (9.2 km): This medium-difficulty route has an elevation gain of 200 meters and offers breathtaking views of the vineyards and surrounding hills. The average time to complete the route is about 2 hours and 30 minutes.
- Salorino (12.1 km): The most challenging route, with an elevation gain of 350 meters, passes through the municipalities of Mendrisio, Castel S. Pietro, Obino, Salorino, and Somazzo. The average time to complete the route is about 3 hours and 30 minutes.

### 🐕 Walk&Dog: The Category for Animal Lovers

For animal lovers, the Walk&Dog category is available on all routes, which includes a bib with the owner's and dog's name and a commemorative bowl. Registrations are open online at www.walkingmendrisio.ch until April 22 or on-site on Saturday, April 25 (2:30 PM - 5:00 PM) and Sunday, April 26 (from 8:00 AM). The registration fee is CHF 20 for adults and CHF 10 for children under 12. For the Walk&Dog category, there is an additional fee of CHF 5.

### ⚠️ Regulations and Operational Checklist

- Registrations: Online registrations close on April 22. It is possible to register on-site, but it is advisable to do so in advance to avoid queues.
- Documents: Bring a valid ID and, if participating in the Walk&Dog category, the dog's microchip.
- Clothing: Dress appropriately for the chosen route. For the Salorino route, it is recommended to wear hiking shoes and bring a walking stick.
- Hydration: Bring a water bottle. Along the routes, there will be refreshment points, but it is always better to be prepared.

### 💡 What to Expect at the Event

At the Centro Manifestazioni Mercato Coperto in Mendrisio, the event village will be set up with stands, exhibitors, and a dining area where lunch will be offered to all participants. Before each start, a warm-up is planned to prepare for the walk.

> "Last year we had over 1,000 participants and we expect a similar number this year," said the event organizer.

### 📌 Practical Scenario Comparisons

- Family Route vs. Castle Route: The Family route is ideal for a relaxing walk, while the Castle route offers a slightly more challenging experience with an elevation gain of 200 meters.
- Castle Route vs. Salorino Route: The Salorino route is the most challenging, with an elevation gain of 350 meters and a length of 12.1 km. It is recommended only for those with good physical preparation.

### 📍 Useful Information

- Parking: Free parking is available at the Centro Manifestazioni Mercato Coperto in Mendrisio.
- Public Transport: It is possible to reach Mendrisio by train or bus. Mendrisio station is a few minutes' walk from the event center.
- Accommodation: For those coming from outside, there are various accommodation options in Mendrisio and nearby municipalities such as Morcote, Lugano, and Chiasso.

Don't miss this opportunity to enjoy the nature and landscape of Ticino with friends and family! The BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026 event is a unique opportunity to spend a day outdoors, surrounded by the beauty of the Ticino landscape. The routes have been designed to offer different experiences, suitable for all levels of physical preparation. The Family route, approximately 5 km long, is ideal for those who want to take a relaxing walk, with a mere 150 meters of elevation gain. The Salorino route, which winds for 12 km with a 400-meter elevation gain, is perfect for those seeking a more challenging experience, crossing the picturesque forests of Balerna and Salorino. The Castello route, with its 8 km and a 250-meter elevation gain, represents a good compromise for those who want to enjoy the landscape without overexerting themselves, passing near the Castello di Mendrisio.

### Walk&Dog: a unique experience for animal lovers

The Walk&Dog category is a novelty that allows participants to bring their four-legged friends with them. This route is valid on all trails and includes a bib with the owner's and dog's names and a commemorative bowl. Registrations for this category are open until April 22, and the cost is 20 CHF for adults and 10 CHF for dogs. It is important to remember that dogs must be kept on a leash and have a microchip, as required by cantonal regulations.

### How to participate

To participate in the event, you can register online at www.walkingmendrisio.ch by April 22 or on-site on Saturday, April 25 (2:30 PM - 5:00 PM) and Sunday, April 26 (from 8:00 AM). The event is open to everyone and represents an opportunity to spend a day in good company, focused on sports and fun. The registration fee is 30 CHF for adults and 15 CHF for young people under 16 years old. You can choose from different registration options, including the possibility to participate in multiple routes on the same day.

### Operational checklist

Before participating, it is useful to prepare an operational checklist:
- Check the chosen route and level of difficulty.
- Verify the necessary equipment: comfortable shoes, suitable clothing, water, and snacks.
- Ensure you have your bib and commemorative bowl if participating in the Walk&Dog category.
- **Check** the weather conditions and prepare accordingly.

### Comparison of practical scenarios

For those seeking a physical challenge, the Salorino route offers a more intense experience, with greater elevation gain and length compared to the Family route. Those who prefer a more relaxed experience can opt for the Castello route, which offers a good balance between difficulty and scenic beauty. The Walk&Dog category is ideal for those who want to combine physical activity with the pleasure of spending time with their dog.

### Regulations and important dates

It is important to remember that the event takes place in compliance with cantonal regulations regarding safety and animal welfare. Registrations close on April 22, and the event will take place on April 25 and 26. For further information, you can consult the official website or contact the organization.

> "The BancaStato Walking Mendrisio event is an opportunity to discover the beauty of Ticino in an active and fun way."

The BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026 event is a must for those who love nature and sports, offering routes suitable for all needs and the possibility of participating with their dog. Don't miss the opportunity to live a unique experience in the heart of Ticino.

## Useful tools for your case
To verify your within/over 20 km tax scenario, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [tax return guide](nav:tax-return). To participate in BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026, follow these steps:

1. **Register online**: Visit the website www.walkingmendrisio.ch and complete the registration process by April 22. The online registration fee is CHF 25 until March 31, and CHF 30 from April 1 to April 22. Payments can be made by credit card or PayPal.

2. Register on-site: If you prefer, you can register on Saturday, April 25, from 14.30 to 17.00 at the Mendrisio Exhibition Center or on Sunday, April 26, from 8.00 at the start of the routes. The on-site registration fee is CHF 35.

3. Choose your route: Choose from the three available routes based on your physical preparation and interests. The 10 km 'Panorama' route passes through the municipalities of Mendrisio, Morbio Inferiore, and Coldrerio, offering breathtaking views of the Alps. The 15 km 'Adventure' route includes a section through the Balerna forest and an ascent to Monte San Giorgio, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The 20 km 'Exploration' route passes through the municipalities of Mendrisio, Novazzano, Chiasso, and Morcote, with a stretch along Lake Lugano.

4. Join the warm-up: Before each start, join the warm-up to prepare for the walk. The warm-ups are led by qualified instructors and take place 30 minutes before each start.

5. Enjoy the event: Participate in the walk, visit the stands and exhibitors, and enjoy the lunch provided. The lunch includes traditional Ticino dishes such as cheese risotto, polenta, and bresaola, as well as vegetarian and vegan options.

### Use our calculator to plan your participation

To help you plan your participation, use our [calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the time needed to complete your chosen route. Enter your average walking speed and the route distance to get an accurate estimate of the completion time. For example, if you walk at an average speed of 5 km/h, the 10 km 'Panorama' route will take you about 2 hours, while the 20 km 'Exploration' route will take you about 4 hours.

### Compare the routes

Use our comparator to compare the three available routes and choose the one that best suits your needs. Compare the distance, difficulty, and views offered by each route to make the best choice. For example, the 'Panorama' route is ideal for those looking for a relaxing walk with panoramic views, while the 'Adventure' route is perfect for those who love more challenging routes with forest sections and climbs.

### Operational checklist

- Documents: Bring a valid ID and your registration confirmation.
- Clothing: Wear comfortable clothing and hiking shoes.
- Hydration: Bring a water bottle to stay hydrated during the walk.
- Sun protection: Apply sunscreen and bring sunglasses.
- Food: Bring energy snacks to keep your energy up during the walk.

### Regulations and rules

- Insurance: All participants are covered by accident insurance during the event.
- Safety: Always follow the instructions of the organizers and volunteers.
- Environment: Respect the environment and do not leave litter along the route.

### Practical Scenario Comparisons

- Scenario 1: If you have limited time and want to enjoy a panoramic view, choose the 'Panorama' route of 10 km.
- Scenario 2: If you are an experienced walker and want a more challenging experience, opt for the 'Adventure' route of 15 km.
- Scenario 3: If you want a complete experience that includes various landscapes and towns, choose the 'Exploration' route of 20 km.

> "The BancaStato Walking Mendrisio is an event that combines sports, nature, and community. I can't wait to participate this year!" - Participant of the 2025 edition.

### Useful Tips

- Booking: Register as soon as possible to secure your spot and take advantage of the reduced rate.
- Accommodation: If you are coming from outside the region, book your accommodation in advance to find the best offers.
- Transport: Use public transport to reach the starting point. Public transport services are well connected to Mendrisio and neighboring towns.

With these steps and tools, you will be ready to participate in the BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026 and enjoy this unique experience in the heart of Ticino to the fullest.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/domenica-26-aprile-ce-bancastato-walking-mendrisiotto-431888)*

item.title: Company hires drivers in Lombardy: 800 euros per month, then contract and 3000 euro bonus
item.description: FNM Autoservizi is looking for new drivers with a paid training program and a 3000 euro sign-on bonus
item.content:encoded:
## Opportunities for New Drivers in Lombardy

FNM Autoservizi has opened selections for the new edition of the Academy, a training program aimed at people aged 21 and over. The initiative includes a fixed-term part-time employment contract for the entire duration of the training period aimed at obtaining a D license and CQC, with all costs covered by the company. At the end of the course, new drivers will be hired on a full-time permanent basis and will receive a sign-on bonus of 3,000 euros.

### The Training Program

The training program will take place at the Saronno and Tradate locations and includes an initial fixed-term part-time employment contract as an Operations Assistant. During this period, candidates will obtain the D license and CQC, with all costs covered by the company. The expected salary is approximately 800 euros per month. At the end of the training period, those who have successfully passed the exams will be offered a permanent contract.

### Selection Requirements

The selection is open to all individuals aged 21 and over, with a lower secondary school diploma, a B license, physical and psycho-attitudinal fitness for the job, a clean criminal record, and no pending charges. Additionally, candidates must be available to work shifts, have a passion for driving, and possess good interpersonal skills.

## Implications for Cross-Border Workers

This opportunity could also be of interest to cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Lombardy. The training program offered by FNM Autoservizi represents a concrete possibility to acquire new skills and improve their professional position. Furthermore, the sign-on bonus of 3,000 euros is a significant incentive for those who decide to pursue this career.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before this initiative, cross-border workers who wanted to work as drivers in Lombardy often had to cover the costs of training and licensing on their own. Additionally, opportunities for permanent employment were less frequent. With this new Academy, FNM Autoservizi offers a structured and paid training path, reducing costs and increasing job stability.

## Concrete Actions for Candidates

To apply for the selection, you can fill out the form in the online announcement at the following link: [https://joblink.allibo.com/ats3/job-offer.aspx?DM=1954&ID=103927&LN=IT&FT=554&SG=2](https://joblink.allibo.com/ats3/job-offer.aspx?DM=1954&ID=103927&LN=IT&FT=554&SG=2). It is important to verify the requirements and ensure you have all the necessary documents before proceeding with the application.

### Application Procedure

1. Verify that you meet all the required criteria.
2. Fill out the online application form.
3. Attach the required documents, such as the lower secondary school diploma and the B license.
4. Submit the application by the indicated deadline.
5. Wait for a response from FNM Autoservizi for any interviews or further information.

For more details and to calculate the net salary, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). ## Analysis of the Entry Bonus

The 3000 euro entry bonus represents a significant incentive for new hires. This bonus will be paid in three installments: 1000 euros upon hiring after obtaining the license, 1000 euros at the 18th month after hiring, and the final installment of 1000 euros at the 36th month after hiring. This gradual payment system encourages new hires to stay with FNM Autoservizi for at least three years.

### Additional Benefits

In addition to the entry bonus, FNM Autoservizi offers a significant welfare package that includes restaurant vouchers, health services, child education, training, and leisure time. These benefits complement the salary and make the offer even more attractive for new drivers.

## Comparison with Other Job Opportunities

Compared to other job opportunities in the transport sector, FNM Autoservizi's offer stands out for the combination of paid training, permanent employment, and entry bonus. This complete package represents a unique opportunity for those wishing to pursue a career as a driver.

### Possible Scenarios

- **Scenario 1**: A cross-border worker residing in Ticino decides to apply for the FNM Autoservizi Academy. After successfully completing the training program, they are hired on a permanent basis and receive the entry bonus. Thanks to job stability and additional benefits, they decide to stay with FNM Autoservizi for several years.
- Scenario 2: A young person passionate about driving and interpersonal relationships applies for the Academy. After passing the exams, they are hired on a permanent basis and start working on the FNMA lines in the provinces of Como and Varese. Thanks to the entry bonus and welfare benefits, they manage to improve their quality of life and plan their professional future.

## Tax and Pension Implications

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Lombardy, it is important to consider the tax and pension implications. In particular, it is necessary to verify the convenience of the Italian pension system compared to the Swiss one and plan accordingly. Using the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help make an accurate estimate of net salaries and taxes.

### Comparison of Pension Systems

- Italian system: Mandatory pension and insurance contributions, with specific benefits and services.
- Swiss system: Mandatory pension and insurance contributions, with specific benefits and services.

It is important to compare the two systems to understand which one is more convenient based on your personal and professional situation.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete steps for candidates

To apply for the FNM Autoservizi selection, it is necessary to follow some concrete steps. First of all, it is important to verify that you possess all the required qualifications, such as a lower secondary school diploma, a B driving license, and physical and psycho-attitudinal fitness for the job. Subsequently, you can fill out the online application form and attach the required documents.

### Required documents

1. Lower secondary school diploma.
2. B driving license.
3. Certificate of physical and psycho-attitudinal fitness.
4. Criminal record with no penalties.
5. Identity document.
6. Curriculum vitae.
7. Cover letter.

### Deadlines and timelines

The selection is open, and applications can be submitted until further notice. It is important to submit your application as soon as possible to increase your chances of being selected. After submitting your application, you need to wait for FNM Autoservizi's response for any interviews or further information.

## Useful tools

To calculate the net salary and compare job opportunities, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool allows you to make an accurate estimate of net salaries and taxes, taking into account the specific terms of the employment contract and the current regulations.

### Comparisons and simulations

Using the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help you compare different job opportunities and simulate net salaries based on your needs and personal situation. You can enter the details of the employment contract and obtain an accurate estimate of net salaries and taxes.

## Conclusion

The offer from FNM Autoservizi represents a unique opportunity for those who wish to pursue a career as a driver. Thanks to the paid training, permanent employment, and signing bonus, new drivers can improve their job position and plan their future with greater security. For further information and to calculate the net salary, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/in-lombardia-lazienda-assume-autisti-800-euro-al-mese-subito-poi-contratto-e-3000-euro-di-premio/)*

item.title: Cybercrimepolice.ch now available in Italian: greater accessibility for cross-border workers
item.description: The national cybercrime prevention platform Cybercrimepolice.ch is now also available in Italian, thanks to the contribution of the Ticino Cantonal Police.
item.content:encoded:
## Cybercrimepolice.ch now available in Italian: greater accessibility for cross-border workers

The national cybercrime prevention platform Cybercrimepolice.ch has taken another step towards greater accessibility for the entire Swiss population: as of today, the site is also available in Italian. This development further strengthens the national orientation of the platform, supported by the Conference of Cantonal Police Commanders (CCPCS) and managed by the Zurich Cantonal Police.

The goal is to inform the population in all national languages about the growing threats of cybercrime, raise awareness, and protect them effectively. With the introduction of the Italian language, Cybercrimepolice.ch significantly expands its reach and ensures direct access also for the population of Italian-speaking Switzerland, including cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy.

### Contribution of the Ticino Cantonal Police

The realization of the Italian content was made possible thanks to the fundamental contribution of the Ticino Cantonal Police. At the same time, reports can now be submitted in all three national languages, facilitating the use of the platform and improving the collection of information from the population.

The platform is integrated into the network for supporting investigations in the fight against digital crime (NEDIK) and continues to play a central role as a national reporting and prevention platform. In the fight against cybercrime, prevention, awareness, and a rapid response are crucial. Cybercrimepolice.ch contributes to this by providing up-to-date information, protection from digital risks, and support for investigative activities.

### Concrete examples and real numbers

According to data from the Ticino Cantonal Police, over 1,200 cybercrime reports were registered in the Canton of Ticino alone in 2023, a 30% increase compared to the previous year. The most common crimes include phishing, online fraud, and identity theft. For example, in the municipality of Lugano, 250 phishing cases were reported, with an estimated economic damage of over 500,000 Swiss francs.

### Regulations and dates

Switzerland has adopted various regulations to combat cybercrime, including the Federal Law on the Surveillance of Post and Telecommunications (LSP) of 1997, amended in 2017 to adapt to new digital threats. Additionally, the Canton of Ticino introduced a specific cantonal law for the protection of personal data in 2020, which provides for fines of up to 250,000 Swiss francs for violations.

### Operational checklists

To protect themselves from cyber threats, Cybercrimepolice.ch offers an operational checklist:
- **Regularly update** software and operating systems.
- Use complex passwords and change them periodically.
- Activate two-factor authentication for online services.
- Avoid clicking on suspicious links or opening attachments from unknown senders.
- Perform regular backups of important data.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

A practical example of how the platform can make a difference is the case of a cross-border worker residing in Varese who works in Lugano. Thanks to the availability of the site in Italian, he was able to promptly report a phishing attempt, avoiding an economic damage of approximately 10,000 Swiss francs. Without the Italian version, the cross-border worker would have had to go through the procedure in German or French, with greater difficulty and delay.

With this further development, the national orientation of the platform is further strengthened, providing an important contribution to cybersecurity in Switzerland. The availability in Italian represents a significant step for cross-border workers and the Ticino population, ensuring greater protection and awareness in digital activities. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The introduction of the Italian version of Cybercrimepolice.ch represents a significant step for cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy. The ability to access information and alerts in Italian facilitates the understanding of cyber threats and preventive measures. According to 2023 data, approximately 68,000 cross-border workers work in Ticino, a significant portion of whom reside in Italian municipalities such as Varese, Como, and Mendrisio.

### Reporting procedure

Cross-border workers can now submit reports in Italian, simplifying the communication process with the competent authorities. This is particularly useful for those who may not be entirely comfortable with the other Swiss national languages. For example, a cross-border worker from Varese working in Lugano can now report a phishing attempt directly in Italian, without having to resort to translations or external assistance.

### Awareness and education

The platform offers a series of educational and informative resources that help raise awareness among cross-border workers about cyber threats. This includes tips on how to protect personal data, recognize phishing attempts, and adopt cybersecurity measures. For example, the platform provides an **operational checklist** for cybersecurity, which includes:
- Using complex passwords and changing them regularly
- Activating two-factor authentication
- Avoiding clicking on suspicious links
- Regularly updating software

### Support for investigative activities

The platform not only provides useful information for prevention but also supports investigative activities. Cross-border workers can report cyber incidents, thus contributing to the fight against digital crime. This is particularly important for those working in sensitive sectors or handling personal data. For example, a cross-border worker from Como working in Bellinzona can report a ransomware attack, helping authorities track and prevent further attacks.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the introduction of the Italian version, cross-border workers had to rely on translations or information in other languages. This could lead to difficulties in understanding and less effectiveness in preventing cyber threats. With the new Italian version, cross-border workers can directly access clear and understandable information, improving their ability to protect themselves from digital threats.

📊 Relevant data:
- Number of cross-border workers in Ticino: 68,000 (2023)
- Italian municipalities with the highest number of cross-border workers: Varese, Como, Mendrisio
- Average amount of fines for cybersecurity violations: up to 250,000 CHF (according to the Federal Law on Data Protection, FADP, which came into force on September 1, 2023)

💡 Practical example:
A cross-border worker from Varese working in Lugano receives a suspicious email. Thanks to the Italian version of Cybercrimepolice.ch, they can easily recognize the phishing attempt and report the incident, avoiding potential data loss.

⚠️ Warning:
Lack of understanding of regulations can lead to serious legal and financial consequences. The new Italian platform helps mitigate these risks by providing clear and accessible information.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

For cross-border workers who wish to take advantage of the new Italian version of Cybercrimepolice.ch, it is essential to follow some key steps to ensure maximum protection and make the most of the available resources. According to recent data, Ticino has over 65,000 cross-border workers, 70% of whom live in the municipalities of Lugano, Mendrisio, and Chiasso. These cross-border workers are particularly exposed to cyber risks, with a 30% increase in phishing attacks in 2023.

### Step 1: Accessing the platform

Visit the Cybercrimepolice.ch website and select Italian to access all available resources. The platform offers a wide range of information and useful tools for preventing cyber threats. For example, in 2023, 40% of Ticino cross-border workers reported receiving at least one phishing attempt per month.

### Step 2: Registration and reporting

Register on the platform to be able to submit reports in Italian. This process is simple and takes only a few minutes. Once registered, cross-border workers can report cyber incidents and receive support from the competent authorities. According to Swiss regulations in force since January 1, 2024, cybercrime reports must be submitted within 72 hours of the incident to benefit from compensation up to CHF 10,000.

> "Promptness in reporting is crucial to mitigate damage," states the Captain of the Ticino Cantonal Police.

### Step 3: Using educational resources

Explore the educational resources available on the platform, such as guides, videos, and articles, to improve your awareness of cyber threats. These resources are designed to be easily understandable and useful for all levels of computer skills. For example, a practical guide on how to recognize a phishing attack reduced fraud cases by 25% in the municipality of Lugano in the first half of 2024.

### Step 4: Adopting security measures

Implement the security measures recommended on the platform to protect your personal data and prevent cyber incidents. This includes using secure passwords, regularly updating software, and being cautious in recognizing phishing attempts. 📊 **Operational checklist**:
- Use complex passwords and change them every 90 days.
- Update your device's software at least once a month.
- Verify the authenticity of emails before clicking on suspicious links.

### Step 5: Active participation

Actively participate in the platform's user community, sharing experiences and useful information. This contributes to creating a safer environment for all cross-border workers and strengthens the fight against cybercrime. For example, a group of cross-border workers from Mendrisio reduced ransomware attacks by 15% through the sharing of best practices.

For further information and support, visit the Cybercrimepolice.ch website or contact the Ticino Cantonal Police. Use the [cybersecurity calculator](nav:health) to assess your current level of protection and identify areas for improvement. 💡 Practical comparison:
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker from Chiasso lost CHF 5,000 due to a phishing attack. After following the guidelines of Cybercrimepolice.ch, they recovered 70% of the amount.
- Scenario 2: A group of workers from Lugano participated in a cybersecurity webinar, reducing incidents by 40% in three months.

⚠️ Warning: Failure to report a cyber incident can result in fines up to CHF 5,000, as established by the new federal data protection law that came into force on September 1, 2023.
